event.summary: Reviewed the bounded proposal and made it claimable without expanding its repositories, paths, or prerequisites.

acceptance.proof: The LSLC-001V PowerShell wrapper ends with exactly one LF and the complete public-baseline-to-corrected-head diff has no whitespace error.
The accepted LSLC-001V receipt remains byte-identical and continues to describe only its original head; the correction has a separate current receipt.
The corrected wrapper still invokes the unchanged focused checker and all V/U/T/O/S evidence gates pass.

The full source-only suite and portable workflow contracts pass at the corrected head with no behavior, dependency, feature, device, runtime effect, socket, network, transport operation, or Manifold authority expansion.

document.commit_policy: Commit one additive validation-only correction after preserving the primary-review failure; do not amend, rebase, rewrite, force-push, or alter accepted LSLC-001V evidence.
document.instruction_none_justification: The correction changes only trailing blank LF bytes in an already routed checker and adds separate workflow evidence; no interface, ownership, activation, validation command, public/private boundary, or routing meaning changes.
non_scope: Any change to LSLC-001V Rust source, fixture, focused Python checker, public documentation, accepted validation receipt, earlier commits, or executed-push receipt.

Dependencies, features, Cargo or project/feature locks, unsafe, native libraries, external implementation inputs, behavior expansion, protocol, wire, discovery, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, or runtime activation.
Devices, interfaces, endpoint semantics, sockets, network access, transport operation, reachability, authorization, or platform effects.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Correct only the extra trailing blank lines in tools/check_lslc_001v.ps1 through a new validation-only unit, preserve the accepted LSLC-001V evidence and published history unchanged, record the primary integration-review failure, and produce fresh exact baseline-diff, owner, workflow, and no-force push evidence for the corrected feature head.
outputs: A PowerShell LSLC-001V wrapper ending in exactly one LF with otherwise identical bytes.
A separate validation receipt bound to the corrected head and explicitly preserving the primary-review whitespace rejection.
Fresh exact public-baseline diff, full owner, workflow, ancestry, remote-readback, and executed-push evidence.
